threads.original_issue_text:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Correct the Python-version conflict evidence**

This conflict table says `AGENTS.md` records Python 3.12 and resolves only `CLAUDE.md` as stale, but the current AGENTS tech stack still says backend Python 3.9 while `pyproject.toml` requires >=3.12. Anyone using this inventory to repair authority drift will ignore one of the real stale authorities and leave the documented runtime target inconsistent; update the table/resolution to name both stale docs or align AGENTS before marking the conflict resolved.

AGENTS.md reference: [AGENTS.md:L80-L80](https://github.com/Zsx154855/NEXARA-PRIME/blob/553d5823d83279efd8f2d71a93b0f2de706158f3/AGENTS.md#L80-L80)

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

threads.original_issue_text:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Derive the receipt link instead of pinning PR23**

This generic runtime-truth validator now hard-codes `.nexara/receipts/pr23_final_attestation.json` and the `pr23_brand_remediation` state block. As soon as a later PR legitimately supersedes this receipt or records provenance under a different state key, `validate_program_state.py` will fail even though the new receipt is the correct canonical one; derive the expected link from the current state/receipt metadata instead of baking in PR #23.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.
threads.actual_fix_description: Generic glob-based receipt discovery, no PR23 hardcode
